sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 22.3g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
sections.subtitleText: 22.3g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 44.2g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a seed, this is a very strong sign.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 17.8g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
sections.subtitleText: 17.8g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 22% daily value stands out most. that is only mild support overall.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin e at 22% daily value stands out most. that is only mild support overall.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 91% daily value, iron at 366% daily value, calcium at 93% daily value stand out most. that is major mineral support.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 91% daily value, iron at 366% daily value, calcium at 93% daily value stand out most. that is major mineral support.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths. often brings dense micronutrient support because that is part of what makes the category interesting. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: spice-like serving sizes limit real payoff because the numbers look huge, but people do not eat 100 grams of cumin seeds. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently. paper scores can overstate realistic serving sizes because people often eat far less than 100 grams.

closing.summary: Cumin Seeds does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.useCaseNote: Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
